commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds admin-only checks for explicit brokered EC2 Mac Dedicated Host pinning, filters portal host inventory to admin or caller-visible attached leases, and updates Worker tests and related docs.

Reproducibility: yes. by source path: current main accepts non-admin hostId lease requests and passes full portal Mac host inventory to portal rendering, and the linked issue documents concrete request steps. I did not run live AWS reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can remove the release-owned changelog entry and align remaining host-pinning docs while leaving the security code path unchanged.

target.title: fix: protect dedicated host capacity
target.body:
## Summary

- require admin authentication for explicit brokered provider-host pinning
- show non-admin portal users only Dedicated Hosts attached to active leases already visible to them
- retain full inventory and unattached host workflows for admins
- update all host-pinning and portal authorization documentation

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/538

## Verification

- `npm run format:check --prefix worker`
- `npm run lint --prefix worker`
- `npm run check --prefix worker`
- `npm run check:node --prefix worker`
- `npm test --prefix worker` (23 files, 647 tests)
- `npm run build --prefix worker`
- `npm run build:node --prefix worker`
- `node scripts/build-docs-site.mjs`
- autoreview: clean

Regression coverage verifies non-admin host-pinned creation stops before provider side effects, admins can still pin, owners see attached hosts, share recipients see only shared attached hosts, and unattached inventory/details remain admin-only.

## Live Worker proof

Ran the production Worker through local Wrangler and sent real HTTP requests to the lease endpoint. No cloud credentials were supplied, so the proof cannot create or spend provider resources.

```text
POST /v1/leases (shared non-admin token, explicit hostId)
403 Forbidden
{"error":"admin_required","message":"provider host pinning requires admin-token auth"}

POST /v1/leases (admin token, same explicit hostId)
424 Failed Dependency
{"error":"provider_not_configured","provider":"aws","missing":["AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID","AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"]}

[wrangler:info] POST /v1/leases 403 Forbidden (16ms)
[wrangler:info] POST /v1/leases 424 Failed Dependency (4ms)
```

The non-admin request fails at the new authorization gate; the admin request passes that gate and reaches provider-readiness validation, with no provider call or paid capacity action.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
